rulings.Case Title: FANX, INC. VS. JAMES DAVIS ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Petition And Petition To Compel Mediation And Arbitrationo
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for November 2, 2023 line 1. PETITION TO COMPEL MEDIATION AND ARBITRATION is GRANTED in part. Cross-Complainant's cause of action for breach of contract shall proceed in arbitration. Cross-Complainant's cause of action for property damage asserted, inter alia, against a third party will proceed in this action. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion For Reconsideration; Recall, Revoke, And/Or Vacate Order Regarding Deft'S Motion Deeming Kriss Miranda A Vexatious Litigant And Prefiling Order
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for November 2, 2023 line 3. PLAINTIFFS' MOTION FOR RECONSIDERATION; RECALL, REVOKE, AND/OR VACATE ORDER REGARDING DEFENDANT'S MOTION DEEMING KRISS MIRANDA A VEXATIOUS LITIGANT AND PREFILING ORDER is DENIED. Plaintiff was declared a vexatious litigant per CCP 391 et seq. upon a regularly noticed motion filed by Defendant (who generally appeared in this action on August 8, 2023 via full scope counsel) on September 1, 2023. Plaintiff chose to file an opposition late (6 court days before the hearing) and then chose to file an unauthorized Supplemental Declaration even later (3:01 a.m. on the date of the hearing). The Court held a hearing on the motion allowing oral argument to be presented; adopted its tentative ruling; and an order tracking the tentative ruling was entered on the same date. Moving party fails to carry its burden under CCP 1008 re: new law, facts or circumstances. The Court notes that the Points and Authorities in support of the Motion are 15-pages long with parts of the brief being single-spaced (see page 11) and parts of the brief being in less than 12-point font (see page 7). Memorandum of Points and Authorities contains not Table of Contents or Table of Authorities. Memorandum of Points and Authorities further appears to contain inaccurate statements regarding procedural posture of cases references in the underlying motion (See page 11 lines 6-9). =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.
